steps.q: Is there a known-full cylinder connected and its valve open?
steps.no: Fit or open a known-full cylinder, then retest.

steps.q: Are the regulator hose and connector secure with no visible damage?
steps.no: Close the cylinder and replace damaged parts before use.

steps.q: Are the individual appliance isolation valves open?
steps.no: Open only the required appliance valve and retest.

steps.yes: The fault is likely local to the remaining appliance.
steps.no: Stop repeated ignition attempts and arrange regulator or system-pressure testing by a competent technician.
Use the No gas appliance works diagnostic first.

steps.q: Is the heater receiving stable 12 V control power?
steps.no: Check the VB04 Heizung 15 A fuse, battery voltage and earth.

steps.q: Is the heater gas isolation valve open and the flue terminal clear?
steps.no: Correct the isolation or obstruction before retrying.

steps.yes: Record the exact code and use the appliance-specific manual.
steps.no: Allow purge time after cylinder changes, then arrange appliance service if ignition still fails.

title: Refrigerator will not run on gas

steps.no: Check cylinder, regulator and manifold supply first.

steps.q: Is the refrigerator gas isolation valve open?
steps.no: Open it and retry according to the appliance instructions.

steps.q: Are the exterior refrigerator vents unobstructed?
Does ignition occur but the flame fail to stay lit?
steps.yes: Burner, thermocouple or flue service is likely required.
steps.no: Check appliance controls and 12 V control supply, then arrange service.

steps.q: Is the burner cap seated correctly and clean?
steps.no: Allow it to cool, clean and reseat it.

steps.q: Do all burners show the same poor flame?
steps.yes: Stop use and arrange regulator-pressure and gas-quality checks.
steps.no: The affected burner needs cleaning or service.

steps.q: Is anyone feeling unwell, dizzy, sick or unusually tired?
steps.yes: Evacuate immediately, call emergency services, and do not re-enter until declared safe.
Turn them off only if safe, evacuate, ventilate from outside where possible, and arrange inspection before reuse.
steps.no: Evacuate, ventilate, check alarm age and arrange investigation before occupying the vehicle.
